title: Market Confidence in Prime Brokerage Risk Management Following the Archegos Capital Management Collapse

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Baseline market confidence in prime brokerage risk controls prior to Archegos exposure disclosure.
Estimated: Slight decline due to early rumors of margin pressure in family offices.
Estimated: Market sentiment stable but cautious.
Estimated: Immediate reaction to the Archegos fire sale and multi-billion dollar bank losses.
Estimated: Continued scrutiny of Credit Suisse and Nomura risk management failures.
Estimated: Regulatory bodies begin formal inquiries into family office transparency.
Estimated: Post-event equilibrium reached with permanent loss of confidence in prime brokerage risk oversight.

events.description: Archegos Capital Management triggers a massive fire sale of stocks, causing significant losses for major global banks.
Credit Suisse announces a $4.7 billion loss related to the Archegos collapse, sparking internal management shakeups.
SEC Chair Gary Gensler signals increased regulatory scrutiny on family offices and derivatives disclosure.

agents.name: Goldman Sachs
agents.description: Prime broker that exited positions early, maintaining high confidence in their own risk management.

agents.name: Credit Suisse
agents.description: Prime broker that suffered massive losses; position shifted to -0.8 post-event.

agents.name: SEC
agents.description: Regulatory body seeking to tighten oversight of opaque financial instruments.

agents.name: Institutional Investors
agents.description: General market participants concerned about counterparty risk and transparency.

agents.name: Bill Hwang
agents.description: Founder of Archegos; represents the high-leverage, high-secrecy investment strategy.

agents.name: Financial Media
agents.description: Outlets like Bloomberg and FT shaping the narrative of systemic risk.

notes: Pro-side: Confidence in existing prime brokerage risk management and transparency. Against-side: Lack of confidence, favoring stricter regulation and oversight of family offices. Data is estimated based on market volatility indices and financial reporting trends during the Q1 2021 period.
